title: 2026 Asia Green Textile Supply Chain Summit to Be Held in Shanghai
author: GreenSupplyHub
excerpt: The 2026 Asia Green Textile Supply Chain Summit, hosted by GreenSupplyHub, is scheduled for June at the Shanghai National Exhibition Center, with over 500 companies expected to participate.
content:
The 2026 Asia Green Textile Supply Chain Summit, hosted by GreenSupplyHub, is scheduled for June at the Shanghai National Exhibition Center. This year's summit focuses on three key themes: carbon neutrality pathways, circular economy model innovation, and digital transformation in the textile industry.

Over 500 textile supply chain companies from China, Japan, South Korea, Southeast Asia and other regions are expected to participate, covering the full industry chain including fiber manufacturing, fabric production, dyeing & finishing, and garment manufacturing. The summit will also feature a "Green Innovation Zone" showcasing the latest eco-friendly textile technologies and products.

title: Impact Analysis: EU New Textile Product Regulations on Asian Suppliers
author: Industry Research
excerpt: The implementation of the EU's Sustainable Textile Products Strategy will have far-reaching implications for Asian textile exporters, requiring early preparation and compliance alignment.
content:
The European Commission's recently released revised Sustainable Textile Products Strategy is set to take full effect in 2027, establishing higher environmental standards across the entire textile product lifecycle.

The new regulations primarily cover: Digital Product Passport (DPP) requirements, microfiber emission control standards, updated restricted chemical substance lists, and mandatory recycling system establishment. For Asian exporters, key areas of concern include supply chain transparency requirements and hazardous chemical management compliance.

GreenSupplyHub recommends that companies begin compliance audits early, with particular focus on ZDHC chemical management, GRS recycling certification, and carbon footprint accounting.

title: Recycled Polyester Market: 2025 Review and 2026 Outlook
author: Market Research
excerpt: The global recycled polyester market reached $18 billion, with Asia contributing over 70% of production capacity. A 15% annual growth rate is projected for the next three years.
content:
According to GreenSupplyHub's latest Asia Recycled Polyester Market Report, the global recycled polyester market reached $18 billion in 2025, with Asia contributing over 70% of production capacity.

China is the largest recycled polyester producer, with annual output exceeding 8 million tons, primarily concentrated in Zhejiang, Jiangsu, and Fujian provinces. As fast fashion brands like SHEIN and ZARA continue to increase their use of recycled materials, global recycled polyester demand is expected to maintain a 15% annual growth rate over the next three years.

Meanwhile, breakthroughs in chemical recycling technology are poised to solve the challenge of blended fabric recycling, bringing new growth momentum to the industry.

title: GreenSupplyHub Welcomes 28 New Certified Member Companies
author: GreenSupplyHub
excerpt: This month the platform added 28 newly verified member companies, including quality suppliers from emerging textile powerhouses such as Vietnam, India, and Bangladesh.
content:
GreenSupplyHub has added 28 new certified member companies this month, bringing total platform membership to over 350. Among the new members, 8 are from Vietnam, 6 from India, 5 from Bangladesh, 4 from China, 3 from Indonesia, and 1 each from South Korea and Japan.

New members span garment manufacturing, fabric weaving, yarn production, and accessories supply, with over 90% holding OEKO-TEX, GRS or other international certifications. The platform will provide these companies with online showcasing, trade matching, and industry intelligence services to help them expand into international markets.

title: Major Breakthrough in Plant Dyeing Technology: Color Fastness Reaches Grade 4-5
author: Technology Editorial
excerpt: A domestic research team has overcome the color fastness challenge in plant dyeing, achieving wash fastness of Grade 4-5 for natural dyes on cotton fabrics.
content:
The China Textile Science Research Institute and Natural Dyeing Technology Co., Ltd. have jointly achieved a major breakthrough in plant dyeing technology. Through innovative mordant formulations and micro-nano processing techniques, natural plant dye wash fastness on cotton fabrics has been elevated from the traditional Grade 2-3 to Grade 4-5, matching the performance of chemical dyeing.

This technological breakthrough means plant-dyed fabrics can now meet international brand quality requirements, potentially propelling natural dyeing from a niche craft to large-scale industrial application. The technology has filed for 3 invention patents and is expected to enter mass production in the second half of 2026.

title: OEKO-TEX Releases 2026 STANDARD 100 Update Highlights
author: Industry Research
excerpt: The OEKO-TEX Association has released the latest version of STANDARD 100, adding PFAS limit requirements and microplastic testing standards.
content:
The International Association for Research and Testing in the Field of Textile and Leather Ecology (OEKO-TEX) has officially released the 2026 edition of STANDARD 100, the most significant revision in five years.

Key changes include: 1) New PFAS (per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances) class-wide limit requirements; 2) Introduction of microplastic fiber release testing standards; 3) Stricter heavy metal limit values; 4) New traceability declaration requirements. Existing certificate holders must complete the transition to the new standard by December 31, 2026.

GreenSupplyHub will host an online seminar next month, inviting OEKO-TEX experts to interpret the new standards and help member companies with a smooth transition.

title: Asia Textile Carbon Neutrality Roadmap Released
author: GreenSupplyHub
excerpt: GreenSupplyHub, in collaboration with multiple institutions, has released the Asia Textile Industry Carbon Neutrality Roadmap, proposing a three-phase approach to achieve industry-wide carbon neutrality by 2050.
content:
GreenSupplyHub, together with the Asia Textile Federation and the China National Textile and Apparel Council, has officially released the Asia Textile Industry Carbon Neutrality Roadmap (2025-2050).

The roadmap proposes three phases: Phase 1 (2025-2030) focuses on promoting clean energy substitution and energy-efficient technology upgrades, targeting a 30% reduction in carbon emission intensity; Phase 2 (2030-2040) involves large-scale deployment of carbon capture technology and circular economy models, targeting a 60% reduction in total emissions; Phase 3 (2040-2050) achieves industry carbon neutrality through carbon sinks and zero-carbon technologies.

The report notes that the textile industry accounts for 4-8% of global emissions, and Asia, as the world's textile manufacturing hub, bears significant responsibility for decarbonization.

title: Ocean Waste Recycled Fiber Project Secures $20 Million Funding
author: Industry Editorial
excerpt: SeaSilk New Materials announces completion of Series B funding, with proceeds to be used for expanding ocean plastic recycling capacity and establishing a new R&D center.
content:
GreenSupplyHub member company SeaSilk New Materials Co., Ltd. has announced the completion of $20 million in Series B funding, led by a renowned international ESG fund with participation from multiple textile industry strategic investors.

The proceeds will be directed toward three areas: first, building a network of ocean waste collection stations across Southeast Asian coastal regions; second, constructing a 5,000-ton annual capacity ocean recycled fiber production line at its Qingdao headquarters; and third, establishing a materials innovation R&D center focused on mixed ocean waste sorting and purification technology.

The company expects to triple its ocean recycled fiber annual output by the end of 2026, meeting growing brand demand.
